key_insight: Instead of protecting individual weights, the paper protects learned concepts in activation space using Sparse Autoencoder (SAE) features, which better preserves knowledge during continual learning in LLMs.
engineering_relevance: For continual fine-tuning of large models, feature-level regularization offers a more selective and memory-efficient alternative to weight-space methods like EWC, improving stability without storing past data.
method: Use pretrained SAEs as a monosemantic feature dictionary for the model’s activations.
Build a compact task-specific feature mask from current-task data and retain only the mask for later training.
Apply an activation-space regularizer that balances stability and plasticity; EWC emerges as a special weight-space special case.
evidence_signal: On TRACE and MedCL continual learning benchmarks, the method outperforms non-architectural baselines, including EWC, and the abstract reports that SAE features separate task-relevant representations far better than the weight basis.
watch_item: The approach depends on a good pretrained SAE and a reliable feature mask, so performance may be sensitive to SAE quality and mask construction.
paper_title: From Weights to Features: SAE-Guided Activation Regularization for LLM Continual Learning
